visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA member state nationals (all EU member states and Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway)
visa_free.notes: As a Schengen member since 31 March 2024 (air/sea) and 1 January 2025 (land), Bulgaria applies Regulation (EU) 2018/1806. EU/EEA/Swiss nationals enjoy full freedom of movement with no visa or stay limit under EU free movement rules.
Swiss nationals enjoy freedom of movement under EU-Switzerland bilateral agreements. No visa required.

visa_policy.notes: Bulgaria is a full Schengen member since 1 January 2025 (land borders; air and sea from 31 March 2024). It applies Regulation (EU) 2018/1806: Annex II nationals are visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period; Annex I nationals require a Schengen visa (Visa C) issued by Bulgarian diplomatic missions. Bulgaria issues uniform Schengen visas valid throughout the Schengen area. No e-visa system exists - all visa applications must be submitted in person at embassies/consulates or Visa Application Centers. The EU Entry/Exit System (EES) became fully operational at Schengen external borders on 10 April 2026. ETIAS is not yet operational as of June 2026 (expected Q4 2026); when ETIAS becomes mandatory, Annex II nationals will require an ETIAS travel authorisation before travel. Vanuatu was transferred from Annex II to Annex I (visa required) effective 4 February 2025 by Regulation (EU) 2025/11. Regulation (EU) 2025/2441 (December 2025) strengthened the visa suspension mechanism under Article 8 but did not modify the Annex I/II country lists. The MFA official page does not enumerate all Annex I/II countries individually; the list above is derived from Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 Annex II (consolidated 30 December 2025) as referenced on the official MFA page.

credential.subtype: uniform (Schengen) visa - Type C or Type D (valid, not expired)

conditional_access.conditions: As of 31 March 2024, nationals of Annex I countries (who would otherwise need a Bulgarian visa) holding a valid uniform Schengen visa may enter and stay in Bulgaria; time spent in Bulgaria counts as part of the Schengen 90/180-day allowance. The Schengen visa must be valid and not exhausted. Applies regardless of whether entry is by air, land, or sea. Source: Bulgarian MFA official page, referencing Art. 2 of Council Decision (EU) 2024/210 and Regulation (EU) 2018/1806.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Algeria do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table of countries whose citizens (holders of diplomatic and service passports) require/do not require visas, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of Armenian diplomatic passports do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Holders of Armenian service passports DO require a visa. Ordinary passport holders also require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Azerbaijan do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of Belarusian diplomatic passports do not require a visa (per Council Decision (EU) 2021/1940 of 9 November 2021 on partial suspension of the EU-Belarus visa facilitation agreement - only specific categories listed therein). Holders of Belarusian service passports DO require a visa. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Cape Verde do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of the People's Republic of China do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Ecuador do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Egypt do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

The European Commission suspended visa-free travel for holders of Georgian diplomatic, service, and official passports effective 6 March 2026 until 6 March 2027 (extendable by up to 24 months), under the reinforced visa suspension mechanism (first application of this mechanism). Ordinary Georgian citizens retaining biometric passports remain visa-free under Annex II of Regulation (EU) 2018/1806. Source: EC press release IP_26_564, 6 March 2026.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of India do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Indonesia do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of Iranian diplomatic and service passports may enter Bulgaria visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Per footnote (1): nationals holding diplomatic and service passports may enter for up to 30 days. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of Jordanian diplomatic passports do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Holders of Jordanian service passports DO require a visa. Ordinary passport holders also require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Kazakhstan do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Kuwait do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Mongolia do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Morocco do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Oman do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Qatar do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of South Africa do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Thailand do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Tunisia do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of Turkish diplomatic and service passports may enter Bulgaria visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Per footnote (1): nationals holding diplomatic and service passports may enter for up to 30 days. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Turkmenistan do not require a visa to enter Bulgaria. Ordinary passport holders require a visa. Source: official MFA table, last updated 14 January 2025.

cbi.notes: Bulgaria's citizenship-by-investment (golden passport) program was officially abolished on 5 April 2022, following a vote of 218 MPs in the Bulgarian National Assembly. The amendment to the Bulgarian Citizenship Act removed the possibility of acquiring citizenship by investing BGN 1,000,000 (approx. EUR 500,000). The program has been closed and no CBI program currently exists.

programs.name: Bulgaria Golden Visa / Permanent Residency by Investment
programs.notes: Under Article 25, paragraph 1, point 6 of the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act, non-EU nationals can obtain permanent residency by investing a minimum of EUR 512,000 (previously expressed as BGN 1,000,000; Bulgaria adopted the euro on 1 January 2026 at a fixed rate of EUR 1 = BGN 1.95583) in Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) or Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) listed on the Bulgarian Stock Exchange. The investment must be maintained for at least 5 years. Permanent residency is granted directly (no temporary permit required first). No minimum physical stay requirement. The Invest Bulgaria Agency (investbulgaria.com) conducts mandatory pre-approval and due diligence. The Financial Supervision Commission (FSC) regulates qualifying investment funds. Processing time is typically 6 to 8 months. After 5 years of permanent residency, investors may apply for Bulgarian citizenship by naturalization. As of January 2026, investments are fully euro-denominated with no currency conversion risk.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals (non-EU/EEA/Swiss) who work remotely for employers or businesses registered outside the EU/EEA/Switzerland. Three eligible categories: (1) employees of companies registered outside EU/EEA/Switzerland, (2) entrepreneurs/managers owning 25%+ of a company registered abroad, (3) self-employed remote workers with at least 1 year of prior remote operation. Minimum average annual income of EUR 31,000 (50x the minimum monthly wage as of January 2025) for the prior calendar year. Must have valid health insurance and proof of accommodation in Bulgaria.
programs.notes: Introduced through amendments to the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act, approved by the Bulgarian Parliament in June 2025. The Council of Ministers adopted the implementing regulations on 10 December 2025 and applications began being accepted from 20 December 2025. The permit is valid for 1 year and may be extended once for an additional year (total maximum 2 years). Holders may not work for Bulgarian companies or clients based in Bulgaria. Administered by the Ministry of Interior.

validation_notes: REMOVED Vanuatu (VUT) from visa_policy.visa_free: Vanuatu was transferred from Annex II to Annex I of Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 by Regulation (EU) 2025/11, effective 4 February 2025. Vanuatu nationals now require a Schengen visa. Source: Council of the EU press release, 12 December 2024; Regulation (EU) 2025/11.

ADDED Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport entry to conditional_access with level=visa_required: The European Commission suspended visa-free travel for holders of Georgian diplomatic, service, and official passports effective 6 March 2026 until 6 March 2027, under the reinforced visa suspension mechanism. Georgian ordinary passport holders retain visa-free access under Annex II. Source: EC press release IP_26_564.

UPDATED Georgia entry in visa_policy.visa_free: Added note clarifying that the diplomatic/service/official passport suspension does not affect ordinary passport holders, and cross-referenced to conditional_access.

UPDATED Kosovo entry in visa_policy.visa_free: Clarified that Kosovo ordinary passport holders have been unconditionally visa-free since 1 January 2024 (the fallback date in the regulation, since ETIAS was not yet operational); added Regulation (EU) 2023/850 as the amendment that added Kosovo to Annex II.

UPDATED Serbia entry in visa_policy.visa_free: Added note that Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 Annex II (M5, September 2024 amendment) clarified Serbia's entry to include passports issued by the Serbian Coordination Directorate.

UPDATED rbi.programs[0].notes: Bulgaria adopted the euro on 1 January 2026 at a fixed rate of EUR 1 = BGN 1.95583. The min_amount remains EUR 512,000 but the previously-cited BGN 1,000,000 denomination is now obsolete. Processing time updated to 6-8 months (consistent with 2026 sources) from the prior '3-8 months' estimate.
UPDATED visa_policy.notes: Added reference to ETIAS not being operational as of June 2026, and noted Vanuatu removal from Annex II.
ADDED ec.europa.eu to official_domains to support Georgia suspension source citation.

2026-06-27: VALIDATION RUN - No changes to visa_policy.visa_free list. Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 Annex II confirmed against consolidated text dated 30 December 2025 (CELEX:02018R1806-20251230): all current visa_free entries remain accurate. Vanuatu correctly absent (removed by Regulation (EU) 2025/11). Regulation (EU) 2025/2441 (December 2025) amended only the suspension mechanism (new Articles 8a-8f), not Annex I or Annex II country lists - no nationality changes needed. UPDATED visa_policy.notes: added EES operational date (10 April 2026) and clarified ETIAS expected Q4 2026; updated reference from consolidated 3 Feb 2025 to 30 Dec 2025. No cbi/rbi/fast_track changes needed - program details confirmed as current. Sources reached: 6 official pages.

2026-06-29: VALIDATION RUN - No changes made. Re-verified against Bulgarian MFA (mfa.bg/en/services-travel/consular-services/travel-bulgaria/visa-bulgaria), EC Home Affairs visa policy page, and web search for 2025-2026 Annex I/II amendments. Findings: (1) No new countries added to or removed from Annex II between the 30 December 2025 consolidation and 29 June 2026. (2) Vanuatu correctly absent (Regulation (EU) 2025/11, effective 4 February 2025 confirmed). (3) Georgia ordinary passport holders confirmed visa-free; only diplomatic/service/official passport suspension (6 March 2026 - 6 March 2027) correctly recorded in conditional_access. (4) Kuwait/Qatar Annex II proposal (COM(2022)189) confirmed still pending - conditional on bilateral visa waiver agreements and ETIAS operational date; both conditions remain unmet as of June 2026. Kuwait and Qatar correctly remain in conditional_access (diplomatic/service only), not visa_free. (5) RBI program investment threshold EUR 512,000 confirmed unchanged. (6) Digital nomad visa EUR 31,000 minimum annual income confirmed (50x minimum monthly salary at program launch). (7) Nicaragua ordinary passport holders: search confirms they remain in Annex II despite reciprocity issues; entry correctly retained in visa_free with caveat note. No nationality-level corrections required. Sources reached: 5 official/semi-official pages.
gaps: The official MFA website (mfa.bg) does not enumerate individual Annex I (visa-required) countries - the site only references Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 without listing them explicitly. The visa-free list was compiled from the EU regulation reference cited on the official MFA page combined with known Annex II contents.
Bulgaria does not operate a standalone e-visa portal (no official evisa.bg or equivalent found). All visa applications are paper-based through diplomatic missions or VFS Global.

ETIAS is not yet operational as of June 2026; expected to launch in Q4 2026. The EU Entry/Exit System (EES) became fully operational at Schengen external borders on 10 April 2026. When ETIAS becomes operative, all Annex II nationals will require an ETIAS travel authorisation for short stays. The exact date ETIAS will become mandatory is not yet confirmed.

The investbulgaria.com official website requires authentication or may be blocked by bot protection; program details cited from law-firm and investment migration secondary sources referencing the Foreigners Act.
The exact processing time for the digital nomad permit and RBI golden visa are not officially published on a government page accessible during this research.

A potentially updated diplomatic/service passport PDF may exist on the MFA website after January 2025 but was not located during this validation; the Georgia suspension (March 2026) may not yet be reflected in any updated MFA PDF.
Dual citizenship policy for naturalized citizens not confirmed from an accessible official source during this research.

Minimum income threshold for the digital nomad permit (EUR 31,000) was based on the minimum wage at time of launch (January 2025); the current minimum wage figure post-euro adoption and potential adjustments were not re-verified from an official source.
The diplomatic/service passport table (mfa.bg PDF, last updated 14 January 2025) does not list agreement dates for individual bilateral visa waiver arrangements.

The Visa Facilitation Agreements page (mfa.bg/en/services-travel/consular-services/travel-bulgaria/visa-facilitation-agreements) may not be accessible publicly; any additional facilitation agreements listed there may not be fully captured.

visa_types.name: Visa A – Airport Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits transit through the international transit areas of Bulgarian airports without entering the Schengen territory. Required for certain nationalities transiting through Sofia Airport.
visa_types.notes: Fee is EUR 80 (approx. USD 87 at 2024 rates) per Regulation (EU) 2019/1155 amending the Schengen Visa Code. Only required for nationals of countries listed in Annex IV of the Schengen Visa Code. Must be applied for at a Bulgarian embassy or consulate in person. Processing standard is 15 calendar days; may extend to 30 days in individual cases.

visa_types.name: Visa C – Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Tourist/Visit)
visa_types.purpose: Permits short-term stays in Bulgaria and the Schengen Area for tourism, family visits, business meetings, cultural events, or transit. Valid for up to 90 days in any 180-day period across all Schengen member states.
visa_types.notes: As of 31 March 2024, Bulgaria issues uniform Schengen visas valid throughout the entire Schengen Area. Fee is EUR 80 (approx. USD 87) per Regulation (EU) 2019/1155; reduced to EUR 40 for children aged 6–12; free for children under 6. Maximum validity is 5 years for multi-entry visas with demonstrated travel history; single and double entry options also available. Applications must be submitted in person at Bulgarian diplomatic missions or authorized Visa Application Centers (VFS Global). Applications accepted no earlier than 6 months before intended travel. Standard processing is 15 calendar days; may extend to 30 days or exceptionally 60 days. The 90-day stay limit is calculated across all Schengen states.

visa_types.name: Visa C – Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Business)
visa_types.purpose: Permits short-term entry for business purposes including negotiations, contract signings, conferences, and commercial visits. Issued as a Type C uniform Schengen visa.
visa_types.notes: Same legal basis and fee as the tourist Type C visa (Schengen Visa Code, Regulation 810/2009 as amended). Applicant must provide an invitation letter from a Bulgarian company or proof of business purpose. Issued as a uniform Schengen visa valid for all Schengen states.

visa_types.name: Visa D – Long-Stay Visa for Employment
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to enter Bulgaria for the purpose of taking up employment with a Bulgarian employer. A prior work permit (issued by the Employment Agency) must be obtained before the visa is issued.
visa_types.notes: Governed by the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act (Article 15). The visa is issued for up to 6 months (180 days) and allows the holder to enter Bulgaria and subsequently apply for a residence permit at the Migration Directorate (MVR). A personal interview is obligatory. The employer must first obtain a work permit from the Bulgarian National Employment Agency. EU Blue Card holders follow a slightly different procedure. After obtaining a Type D visa and entering Bulgaria, the applicant must apply for a temporary residence permit within the first month. No e-visa option exists; applications must be submitted in person.

visa_types.name: Visa D – Long-Stay Visa for Study
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals admitted to an accredited Bulgarian educational institution to enter Bulgaria to pursue studies. Includes university degree programs, language courses, and vocational training.
visa_types.notes: Issued under the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act. Requires an acceptance letter from an accredited Bulgarian institution, proof of sufficient funds, and health insurance. A personal interview is obligatory. After entering Bulgaria on a Type D study visa, students must register with the Migration Directorate (MVR) to obtain a residence permit valid for the duration of their studies. Students may work limited hours under Bulgarian labor law.

visa_types.name: Visa D – Long-Stay Visa for Family Reunification
visa_types.purpose: Permits family members of Bulgarian citizens, EU/EEA nationals, or third-country nationals legally residing in Bulgaria to enter and reside in Bulgaria for the purpose of family reunification.
visa_types.notes: Governed by the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act and implementing the EU Family Reunification Directive (2003/86/EC) for third-country national sponsors. Eligible family members include spouse, minor children, and dependent parents. A personal interview is obligatory. After entry, family members must apply for a residence permit at the Migration Directorate (MVR). Spouses of Bulgarian nationals may follow an expedited path to permanent residency.

visa_types.name: Visa D – Long-Stay Visa for Investment (Golden Visa path)
visa_types.purpose: Permits non-EU nationals who have made qualifying investments in Bulgaria (such as EUR 512,000 in approved investment funds) to enter Bulgaria. Leads directly to permanent residency without requiring a temporary permit.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Article 25, paragraph 1, point 6 of the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act. Requires a minimum investment of EUR 512,000 in Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) or ETFs listed on the Bulgarian Stock Exchange, maintained for at least 5 years. The Invest Bulgaria Agency (investbulgaria.com) must pre-approve the investment. After approval, a Type D visa is issued to allow entry, following which permanent residency is granted directly. After 5 years of permanent residency, the investor may apply for Bulgarian citizenship by naturalization. Bulgaria's Citizenship-by-Investment (golden passport) program was abolished on 5 April 2022; this path leads to residency only, not direct citizenship.

visa_types.name: Visa D – Long-Stay Visa for Scientific Research / Highly Qualified Employment (EU Blue Card)
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals employed in highly qualified occupations (requiring at least a bachelor's degree and a gross annual salary at least 1.5x the average Bulgarian salary) to enter Bulgaria under the EU Blue Card scheme.
visa_types.notes: Implements Directive 2009/50/EC (EU Blue Card Directive) and its successor Directive (EU) 2021/1883 as transposed into Bulgarian law. The employer must apply for an EU Blue Card (combined work and residence permit) from the Migration Directorate on behalf of the foreign national. A Type D entry visa is issued to allow initial entry. Blue Card holders have enhanced mobility rights within the EU/Schengen Area after 18 months.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Residence Permit (Type D visa + residence permit)
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals who work remotely for employers or businesses registered outside the EU/EEA/Switzerland to reside in Bulgaria for up to 2 years without taking up local employment.
visa_types.notes: Introduced by amendments to the Foreigners in the Republic of Bulgaria Act, approved by the Bulgarian Parliament in June 2025. Implementing regulations adopted by the Council of Ministers on 10 December 2025; applications accepted from 20 December 2025. Three eligible categories: (1) employees of companies registered outside EU/EEA/Switzerland; (2) entrepreneurs/managers owning 25%+ of a company registered abroad; (3) self-employed remote workers with at least 1 year of prior remote operation. Minimum average annual income of EUR 31,000 for the prior calendar year. The initial permit is valid for 1 year, extendable once for a further year (maximum 2 years total). Holders may not work for Bulgarian companies or Bulgarian clients. Administered by the Ministry of Interior (MVR). Entry is via a Type D long-stay visa; the permit is then issued inside Bulgaria. Processing time is not officially published.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official / Service Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports of countries that have bilateral agreements with Bulgaria requiring visas for such passport holders. For diplomatic passport holders of many countries, no visa is required (see conditional_access).
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic and service passport holders of many countries are exempt from visa requirements per bilateral agreements (see conditional_access entries in this file). Where a visa is required for diplomatic/service passports, it is typically issued free of charge and through diplomatic channels with expedited processing. The official MFA table (last updated 14 January 2025) lists which nationalities require or do not require visas when holding diplomatic/service passports.

visa_free.name: EU/EEA member states (all 27 EU members + Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway)

outbound_official.notes: The official Bulgarian MFA outbound visa tool is the interactive Travel Assistant at https://www.mfa.bg/en/63 (titled 'Do I need a visa for my trip?'), which provides country-by-country visa requirements for Bulgarian ordinary-passport holders. The tool covers approximately 194 countries. The site is served behind Radware bot-manager protection (HTTP 302 to validate.perfdrive.com) and cannot be scraped programmatically; its content is a dynamic country-picker, not a flat list. The visa_free list above is derived from Bulgaria's membership in the EU/Schengen area and Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 Annex II (the same regulation Bulgaria applies as a full Schengen member since 1 January 2025), which the MFA Travel Assistant references. As a full Schengen Area member, Bulgarian ordinary-passport holders enjoy freedom of movement within all EU/EEA states and visa-free access to all Annex II third countries under Schengen mutual benefit. The MFA also maintains country-specific travel pages at https://www.mfa.bg/en/embassyinfo/<country> for approximately 194 destinations. No asof date could be extracted due to bot protection.
